summary: date-uuid ships an import-time dropper that downloads and executes a remote.vbs payload
details: On require()/import, index.js loads lib/bootstrap.js which invokes extractDateISO(). The function reconstructs a remote URL from an AES-256-GCM-sealed ciphertext whose plaintext is completed by two characters read from lines 115/116 of the consumer's README (PORT=/HOST= tokens). It then POSTs `${os.platform()} ${os.release()}` to that URL, downloads the response body, writes it to `tmpdir()/temp_<timestamp>.vbs` (with the `.vbs` extension built via string concatenation `'v'+'b'+'s'` to evade naive scans), and executes the file via child_process.exec. This behavior is unrelated to the package's advertised UUID v7 utility purpose and constitutes an import-time remote code execution dropper. Host OS/release fingerprint is also transmitted to the attacker-controlled endpoint before the payload fetch.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
